cases.title: Assigned Lecturer closes Open session and blocks new student check-in
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to the target class section.
A class session exists in Open state with active QR tokens and enrolled students.
At least one enrolled student has not yet checked in.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with Lecturer Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard {data, meta, error} envelope.
Assert data.state is Closed, closedAt is non-null ISO-8601 UTC, and data.summary contains present/late/manualPresent/absent counts.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and verify token is no longer Valid or request is rejected.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as an enrolled student using a token from the closed session.
Assert check-in is rejected with error.code SessionClosed and no new successful attendance record is created.
cases.expected: WF-06 / E2E-07 happy path: session transitions Open to Closed, close summary is returned, QR tokens are invalidated, and subsequent student check-in is rejected with SessionClosed per BR-02 and AC-05.

cases.title: Testing-plan session open/close row: close blocks self check-in after transition
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to section is authenticated.
Session fixture is Open with enrolled students per testing-plan §3.1 Session open/close coverage block.
Student account and valid QR token fixture are available while session is Open.
steps: While session is Open, submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student and confirm check-in is not rejected with SessionClosed.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Closed with closedAt and summary per AC-05 Given/When/Then.
Re-submit POST /v1/check-ins with the same or a previously valid QR token.
Assert error.code SessionClosed and verify no new Present/Late success row is created.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and assert data.state is Closed.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan Session open/close row: pre-close check-in succeeds or proceeds normally; close succeeds with Closed metadata; post-close check-in fails with SessionClosed and no new success attendance record per AC-05.

cases.title: M03 Session Lifecycle atomically persists Closed state with closedAt and closedByUserId
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for class_sessions table.
An Open session row exists with known sessionId and assigned lecturerUserId on the parent section.
Lecturer auth context matches the section assignment.
steps: Invoke M03 closeSession(sessionId, actor) or POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close through API service layer.
Query class_sessions table and assert state = Closed, closed_at is non-null, and closed_by_user_id equals the Lecturer actor.
Invoke getSessionState(sessionId) and assert Closed with no further open transition possible.
Verify active QRSessionToken rows for the session are invalidated or session-state guard rejects token validation.
Submit check-in through M04 for an enrolled student and assert SessionClosed outcome without AttendanceRecord success insert.
cases.expected: M03 module + repository integration commits state Closed, closedAt, and closedByUserId atomically; token invalidation and check-in rejection follow without partial close state.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close returns 200 envelope with Closed payload and summary
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Target session is in Open state with enrolled students and mixed attendance outcomes.
Request includes Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers per API design §2.2.
steps: Send POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with optional empty body.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error} with error null.
Assert data contains classSessionId, state Closed, closedAt, and summary object with present, late, manualPresent, and absent integer counts.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp fields.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; data reflects Closed session with closedAt and attendance summary per API design §4.3.

cases.title: Only Open pre-state transitions to Closed; Scheduled, Closed, and Cancelled reject close
preconditions: Session fixtures exist in Scheduled, Open, Closed, and Cancelled states.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is available for all attempts.
steps: Record beforeState Open and invoke close for Open session; record afterState Closed.
Attempt close on session in Scheduled state.
Attempt close on session already in Closed state.
Attempt close on Cancelled session.
Query persistence after each rejected or idempotent attempt and confirm state unchanged or idempotent Closed result only.
cases.expected: beforeState Open transitions to afterState Closed; Scheduled and Cancelled pre-states reject with invalid-transition behavior (409 InvalidSessionTransition); Closed pre-state returns idempotent closed summary without duplicate side effects per SM-01.

cases.title: Non-assigned and non-lecturer roles are denied SessionControl.execute close
preconditions: Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer-not-assigned-to-section test accounts exist with valid tokens.
An Open session exists in a section the out-of-scope Lecturer is not assigned to.
AcademicAdmin token is available as positive control for override permission.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as Student.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code (expect 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope) for each denied request.
Query class_sessions table to confirm session state remains Open.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as AcademicAdmin and assert override succeeds per permission matrix §3.1.
cases.expected: Student, ITAdmin, and out-of-scope Lecturer receive 403 without state change; AcademicAdmin override may close per roles doc; assigned Lecturer remains primary actor for FR-08.

cases.title: Close on Scheduled or Cancelled session returns InvalidSessionTransition
preconditions: Scheduled and Cancelled session fixtures exist with known sessionIds.
Assigned Lecturer and AcademicAdmin tokens are available.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/close as assigned Lecturer.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{cancelledSessionId}/close as assigned Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each response.
Verify class_sessions rows remain Scheduled and Cancelled respectively with no closedAt set.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 409 with error.code InvalidSessionTransition for Scheduled and Cancelled targets; FR-08 close is only valid from Open state.

cases.title: System auto-closes Open session when policy late window elapses
preconditions: Open session exists with effective policy defining auto-close after late window end.
Test harness can advance clock or trigger scheduler evaluation past late window.
Lecturer has not manually closed the session.
steps: Open session and record openedAt and policy late window end timestamp.
Advance time past late window end or invoke auto-close scheduler job.
Query session state and assert Closed with closedAt set and closedByUserId indicating system actor if applicable.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student and assert SessionClosed rejection.
Verify GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current no longer returns Valid token.
cases.expected: Alternate branch per BR-21: system transitions Open to Closed when policy late window elapses; same post-close check-in blocking and token invalidation as manual close.

cases.title: Concurrent close and in-flight check-in resolve against committed session state
preconditions: Open session exists with enrolled student and valid QR token.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close and POST /v1/check-ins.
Assigned Lecturer and Student auth contexts are established.
steps: Submit parallel close command and student check-in request against the same Open session.
Collect HTTP outcomes for both requests.
Query class_sessions table and assert exactly one Closed transition with single closedAt.
Verify check-in attempt outcome is either Success (if evaluated before close commit) or SessionClosed (if evaluated after close commit) with no duplicate success rows.
Assert at most one successful AttendanceRecord per student-session pair.
cases.expected: Close overlaps check-in per testing-plan §7.2: committed session state at validation determines Success or SessionClosed; no duplicate success records or illegal dual transitions.

cases.title: Student check-in after close returns SessionClosed with 422 rule-failure envelope
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer and enrolled Student accounts are authenticated.
A class session has been opened then closed by the assigned Lecturer.
Student has no prior successful attendance record for the session.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken bound to the closed session.
Inspect HTTP status and response envelope fields data/meta/error.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed with user-facing message key present.
Query attendance records and CheckInAttempt rows for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Student request is rejected as a rule failure with error.code SessionClosed in the standard envelope; failed CheckInAttempt is logged with SessionClosed outcome and no Present/Late success row is created per AC-05.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance reflects Closed state after close
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Open session with enrollments and mixed check-in outcomes exists.
Session has not yet been closed.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close and capture closedAt and summary from response.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization.
Validate HTTP 200 and envelope shape.
Assert data.state is Closed, counts object includes present/late/pending/rejectedAttempts keys consistent with close summary, and rows array reflects final roster shape per API design §4.5.
cases.expected: Attendance roster endpoint returns HTTP 200 with data.state Closed and finalized roster payload immediately after FR-08 close; live dashboard transitions to closed roster view per FR-19.

cases.title: Lecturer PG-04/PG-05 journey closes Open session and shows Closed badge with QR disabled
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Assigned section has at least one Open session visible on PG-04 session list.
QR panel is active on session detail page.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions (PG-04) and locate an Open session row with Open status badge.
Select the session and navigate to session detail / QR display (PG-05).
Tap or click Close Attendance and observe loading then success feedback with close summary.
Verify session badge transitions to Closed, closedAt indicator appears, and QR panel is hidden or shows closed-state messaging.
Confirm Close CTA is disabled and view-roster or export actions reflect closed session per UI states §2.
Optionally verify student check-in page shows SessionClosed-equivalent error if navigated after closure.
cases.expected: Browser journey completes WF-06 UI path: session transitions Open to Closed with closedAt metadata, QR display stops, and state-appropriate control gating prevents further self-service check-in.
